zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Anne Devereux於大約1413年出生在英國賀福爾波登哈姆時，她的父親Walter Devereux27歲，母親Elizabeth Maud Bromwich34歲。她於大約1429年與John Milbourne在英國賀福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1475年在英國賀福爾波吉爾享年63歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾波吉爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Anne Devereux于大约1413年出生在英国賀福爾波登哈姆时，她的父亲Walter Devereux27岁，母亲Elizabeth Maud Bromwich34岁。她于大约1429年与John Milbourne在英国賀福爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1475年在英国賀福爾波吉爾享年63岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾波吉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Devereux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Sean.
English and Irish: of Norman origin, a habitational name for someone from Évreux in Eure, France, with the preposition d(e). See also Everest for the name without the preposition.

History: This was the surname of the Earls of Essex from 1572 to 1641. Walter Devereux, 1st Earl of Essex, led an attempted colonization of Ulster, and his son, Robert, 2nd Earl of Essex, briefly held the position of lord lieutenant of Ireland in 1599.

variants.name: Devereaux
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Everest
meanings: English (southeastern): of Norman origin, a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de). Compare Evers .

variants.name: Evers
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de).

English: de-aspirated variant of Heaver, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a habitational name from Hever (Kent), named from Old English hēah ‘high’ + (Kentish) Old English ēfre ‘edge or brow of a hill’. Alternatively, the name may derive from some minor place in southeastern England named with Old English ȳfre, (Kentish) Old English ēfre, Middle English evere ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, with the addition of a prosthetic H-.
North German and Dutch: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Evert , which lost its t in pronunciation and spelling subsequently; compare Everts . Compare also Avers .

variants.name: Deveraux
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devery
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Kieran, Brendan, Clancy.
Irish (Offaly): of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devary
meanings: Probably Irish: variant of Devery, a variant of Devereux in counties Leix and Offaly.

variants.name: Debrow
meanings: Altered form of English or Irish   Devereux  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Anne Devereux was born about 1413, in Bodenham, Herefordshire, England, her father, Walter Devereux, was 27 and her mother, Elizabeth Maud Bromwich, was 34. She married John Milbourne about 1429, in Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1475, in Burghill, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Burghill, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Anne Devereux est née environ 1413, à Bodenham, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter Devereux, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Maud Bromwich, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé John Milbourne environ 1429, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1475, à Burghill, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Burghill, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Anne Devereux um 1413 in Bodenham, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter Devereux 27 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Maud Bromwich 34. Sie hat um 1429 in Herefordshire, England John Milbourne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1475 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Burghill, Herefordshire, England und wurde in Burghill, Herefordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Anne Devereux è nata il circa 1413 nel Bodenham, Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Walter Devereux, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Maud Bromwich, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato John Milbourne circa 1429, nel Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1475, nel Burghill, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolta a Burghill, Herefordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Anne Devereuxが約1413年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアボデンハムで生まれた時，父Walter Devereuxは27歳，母Elizabeth Maud Bromwichは34歳であった。約1429年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでJohn Milbourneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1475年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブルギルで63歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブルギルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Anne Devereux(이)가 대략 1413년 영국 허퍼드실르 보데함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter Devereux(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Maud Bromwich(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1429년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 John Milbourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1475년 영국 허퍼드실르 버그힐에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 버그힐에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Anne Devereux nasceu aproximadamente 1413, em Bodenham, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter Devereux, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Maud Bromwich, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com John Milbourne aproximadamente 1429, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1475, em Burghill, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultada em Burghill, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Anne Devereux родилась около 1413 г. в местности боденам, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter Devereux было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Maud Bromwich, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Milbourne около 1429 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1475 г. в местности бургилл, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности бургилл, хирефордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Anne Devereux nació aproximadamente 1413, en Bodenham, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter Devereux, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Maud Bromwich, tenía 34 años. Se casó con John Milbourne aproximadamente 1429, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1475, en Burghill, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en Burghill, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
